observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor order the GSO-Designate to maintain records regarding acquisition, description, custody, estimated life, rate of depreciation, disposal, and other information of PPE based on the source documents of the transactions and reconcile with the Accounting Records.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall update the record of acquisition/disposal/transfer of PPEs in the PPELCs and ensure that the total balance of PPELCs tally with the balances of controlling PPE accounts in the General Ledger.
We further recommended that the Inventory Committee, in coordination with the GSO and Municipal Accountant, plan/strategize on how to conduct and complete the physical inventory within the prescribed period for the purpose of one-time cleansing of the PPE accounts, and to undertake the disposition of the non-existing PPEs and unserviceable PPEs in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Require the Municipal Assessor to:
prepare and submit to the Municipal Treasurer, on or before the thirty-first (31st) day of December each year, an assessment roll containing a list of all persons whose real properties have been newly assessed or reassessed and the values of such properties pursuant to RA No. 7160; and
verify and determine whether all real properties within the province are properly listed in the assessment roll and update and reconcile all records pertaining to real properties within the Municipality and its owners.
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to:
to update the Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index card based on the updated Assessment Roll submitted by the Municipal Assessor.
furnish the Municipal Accountant of a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible at the beginning of the year based on the updated Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index card.
furnish the Municipal Accountant of a copy of the List of the RPT Delinquents at the end of the year.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to:
record every January of each year the full amount of RPT due and collectible as receivables based on the computed amount per RPT Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index cards; and
reconcile the RPT/SET Receivables accounts with the Municipal Treasurer’s list of RPT Delinquents. Prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries, if any, in order to present the correct balance of the account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a)    trace prior years balances of accounts payable and the correctness of accounting entries on its recognition and settlement, and make the appropriate adjusting journal entry, as necessary; and
b)    stop the practice of recording accounts payable without complete supporting documents.  Henceforth, JEV on the recognition of payables should be consummated transactions supported with complete documents to prove that these are valid liabilities.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor secure written agreement for every undertaking involving fiscal administration and direct the:
MTO to provide the Municipal Accountant with a copy of the Lease Contract of all the tenants and the Monthly Consumer Account Reports for the Waterworks System as the basis for the recording of the revenue.
Municipal Engineer to conduct an inspection of the repairs made on the unit to determine the reasonableness of the cost and the extent of the repairs which the Municipality should shoulder based on the existing contract, and submit to the Municipal Accountant the report indicating therein the amount to be charged to the Municipality.
Municipal Accountant to record the income on an accrual basis, and prepare subsidiary ledger per tenant. Also, conduct monthly reconciliation with the MTO records to ensure check and balance and to allow immediate detection and correction of errors. And, to record the repairs made on the Commercial Building based on the report submitted by the Municipal Engineer and the supporting receipt.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor implement acquisition of lot for relocation site to utilize the fund assistance from the Office of the President, and immediately refund any unexpended balance to the source agency after project completion.
We also recommended that:

the Municipal Treasurer refund the unexpended fund transfers to their respective source agencies, and immediate reversion of  the Congressional Funds that was declared unconstitutional by the Supreme Court.
the Accountant prepare the aging of fund transfers using the format required in Item 7.4 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 and provide disclosures on dormant balances, if any, in the Notes to Financial Statements, and
request for write-off the dormant fund transfers, if warranted, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Disbursing Officer to liquidate the Cash Advances as soon as the purpose for which it was given has been served or within the period prescribed by COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997, to avoid the accumulation of the accountability which may exceed the amount covered by the fidelity bond.
Also, direct the Disbursing Officer to reconcile the book balance with the cash on hand daily.  Foot and close the books at the end of each month and to provide the certification in the cashbook after the end of each month.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Local Development Council and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, identify specific projects to be funded from the 20% Development Fund in the Annual Investment Plan and to avoid lump sum appropriation containing generic or general terms.
We also recommended that the Mayor, through the Bids and Awards Committee, secure approval or authority from the Sangguniang Bayan to enter into contract prior to entering a contract with a winning bidder in cases of lump sum appropriation containing generic or general terms or authority to charge under the 20% Development Fund.
Lastly, we recommended the Municipal Budget Officer to include in the annual budget preparation the review of continuing appropriation unexpended balances that are no longer needed so that these could be reverted to the unappropriated surplus and re-appropriated to other PPAs in order to prevent idle funds.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the:
The Municipal Accountant to transfer all of the unexpended balance of the MOOE and QRF of the current appropriation for DRRM to the STF-DRRM fund, and not to include the PPAs under the capital outlay and the unexpended balance of the continuing appropriation for the DRRM.
The MDRRMO to prepare a Supplemental LDRRMFIP to incorporate therein the prioritized PPAs and the updated total resource requirements for PPAs not included in the annual budget, for approval by the DRRM Council.
The MPDC to prepare a supplemental Annual Investment Program to incorporate the new prioritized PPAs and resource requirements based on the approved LDRRMFIP and secure approval from the Sangguniang Bayan.
The Budget Officer to effect changes in the annual budget for DRRM based on the approved AIP, and avoid processing budget realignment without the approved AIP and LDRRMFIP for the DRRM Funds.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the:
Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Budget Officer a copy of the RCD or credit memo of every cash donation received for DRRM.
The Municipal Budget Officer to prepare RCDU per donor and per purpose, and timely update the registry by posting the transactions based on the source documents such as the RCD for the receipt and the CDUR for its utilization.
The Municipal Accountant to;
Transfer the recorded cash donations from the Regular Trust Fund to the Trust Fund-DRRM books.
Include in the LDRRM Utilization Report the receipt and utilization of the cash donations for DRRM.
Require the CDUR, duly signed by the head of the requesting unit and the budget officer, as attachment to the Disbursement Voucher on the utilization of cash donations.
Disclose all relevant information not evident on the face of the FS, such as but not limited to the donation received, utilization, and balances in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the:
MDRRMO to conduct collaborative planning with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator in the prioritization of the PPAs prescribed under the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan to avoid the appropriation of unimplementable PPAs.
MDRRMO to fast-track and monitor the implementation of the appropriated PPAs and the re-channeling of funds of PPAs that could no longer be implemented to other prioritized PPAs as incorporated in the LDRRMFIP and AIP.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Require the HRMO to assess the existing Organizational Structure so that the ideal number of plantilla personnel will be established and submit a report with recommendations thereon to the Local Chief Executive;
Create clear-cut policies and guidelines to address the issue on the necessity and propriety of hiring JOs taking into consideration the existing regular employees; and
Comply with the existing daily minimum wage rate for Region VIII, as prescribed by the RTWPB.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Public Health Nurse as Vaccine Coordinator to:
Develop and/or enhance measures that will provide for the swift administration of the vaccines to the eligible population such as but not limited to putting a premium on communications and advocacy to boost the confidence and acceptance of the vaccine;
Provide for prior registration of persons willing to be vaccinated to determine the demand for the vaccines, and not to request nor accept  vaccines that cannot be inoculated before the expiry.
Develop an inspection process prior to acceptance of vaccines and timelines to avoid the incidence of expiration of vaccines.

We commended the Municipal Mayor and all personnel concerned in ensuring that identified development projects and utilizations charged to the Development Fund were in adherence to the provisions of Item 3.2.5 of DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1, dated November 4, 2020.
We likewise recommended the Management to continually make a conscious effort to accomplish the identified development projects efficiently and effectively in accordance with rules and regulations.

We recommended that:
The Municipal Treasurers to prepare at year-end a List of Unreleased Checks to be attached to the Report of Checks Issued and submit to the Municipal Accountant and to COA Auditor.

The Municipal Accountant should restore unreleased checks by preparing a journal entry voucher at the end of the year based on the list submitted from the MTO. At the start of the ensuing year, draw another entry to reverse the previous entry made and recognize the availability of the checks for release.

We also recommended to make representation with the bank to ask for alternative remedy and exhaust all other means to correct the error made by the Bank.

income of the Municipality after allocating full provision for statutory and
contractual obligations. They should conduct self-assessment of the
Municipality’s capacity to repay the loan taking into consideration the net
available funds, ensure that utilization of loan proceeds is covered by
appropriation ordinance, determine the most cost effective borrowing
scheme, contract loans only upon proper evaluation of priority projects, and
allocate sufficient funds to cover total contracted liabilities.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Engineer:

to ensure that all conditions preliminary to the commencement of
construction have been addressed and that a certification shall be
executed that detailed preliminary engineering has been conducted prior
to the start of procurement activities to ensure that necessary
requirements and documents have been undertaken for the project to be
completed as planned;
to review thoroughly the request for time extension by contractors based
on complete and relevant documents that will enable the assessment of
the reasonableness of the reasons cited;
to specify in the notice of suspension of work the period within which work
is suspended and the reason for granting the request to facilitate
monitoring;
to ensure the timely issuance of notice of resumption of work, so work is
started by the contractors immediately after the period of work
suspension;
to maintain complete and orderly files of documents pertaining to project
implementation such as approved contract time extensions, notices of
suspension of work and resumption of work to facilitate review and
monitoring, and to ensure that appropriate action are acted on time; and,
to submit to the Audit Team the Approved Contract Time Extension and
its supporting approved notices of suspension of work and resumption of
work

We further recommended to the Municipal Mayor to:
direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) (a) to properly plan the
procurement of infrastructure projects; (b) to initiate the forfeiture of
Performance Security of erring contractors in favor of the Municipality in
case of non-completion of projects within the contract period; and (c) to
initiate the sanction of blacklisting erring contractors, if warranted.
require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to see to it
that liquidated damages will be effected on every day of delay until the
project is completed and accepted by the officials concerned.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal DRRM
Officer to prepare and submit the required reports pursuant to RA No. 10121
and COA Circular No. 2012-002.

• locate all the source documents and MOA with the source agencies to
review the terms and conditions corresponding to the funds transferred in
relation to the implementation of programs and projects;
• determine if the specific programs or projects for which the funds were
transferred were already implemented, goods/services were already
delivered, liquidation of funds were already submitted and received by the
source agencies, and liabilities were already settled for proper
adjustments in the books;
• return to the source agency any unused/unexpended balance if so
stipulated in the MOA;
• request authority for the write off of dormant fund transfer accounts in
accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016, if
warranted, duly supported by a quarterly aging of dormant fund transfers
and other relevant documents.

• cause the creation of policies and guidelines to address the issue on the
necessity and propriety of hiring JO workers taking into consideration the
existing regular employees.
• limit the hiring of personnel under Job Order contracts to those extremely
necessary and within the age limit, and deploy only to offices that actually
are in need of their services.
• direct the Human Resource Officer to require Job Order workers to submit
primary personal identification, undergo interview and evaluate the
performance of each to give assurance that those hired have the skills
required by the office.
• direct the Human Resource Officer to incorporate in the contract the
number of man hours, quantity and duration of the work to be performed
by the JO worker and require them to submit quantified accomplishment
reports to support payment of wages.
• cause the reduction of number of job-order workers to accommodate the
increase of daily rate pursuant to the minimum wage required under
Wage Oder No. RB VIII-21.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to cause the issuance of an
Executive Order creating an Oversight Committee in compliance with DILG-
DOF JMC No. 2019-01 dated May 17, 2019, which will review the current
Revenue Code of the Municipality and recommend revisions thereon for
submission to the Sangguniang Bayan.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to:
a) require the Municipal Treasurer, PUEEO In-charge and other members of
the Local Finance Committee to review the provisions in the Contract of
Lease and make the necessary revisions thereon especially on penalties of
delinquent lessees.
b) cause the execution of Water Service Contracts to its concessionaires
stipulating the terms, conditions and penalties in case of non-payments.
Secondly, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
a) issue demand letters to delinquent market lessees;
b) issue notices to vacate for failure to pay the rent after the issuance of demand
letters.
Lastly, we recommended that the PUEEO In-charge:
a) continue to monitor the monthly collections of the billed water consumption
for the Office and take proper action on non-paying consumers; and
b) issue demand letters to delinquent water service concessionaires and
exercise the right to disconnect service lines for reasons of failure to comply
with the demand letter within the stipulated period.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the GAD Focal Person to
conduct gender analysis in the formulation of programs and projects and
activities so that the intended beneficiaries are assured of the services,
protection and other assistance that may be provided.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the officials concerned to
formulate a separate plan and programs using 1% of the total budget
intended to address the concerns of Senior Citizens and Persons with
Disability.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to cause the licensing of its
ambulance with the DOH.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal
Environmental and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO) to coordinate with
the Southern Leyte Cooperative (SOLECO) to fast-track the installation of
electrification at the Municipal MRF to ensure utilization of the acquired
machineries.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor and the local sanggunian to:
a. craft ordinances and issue executive orders related to waste
management and environmental protection;
b. strengthen the current waste management system of the Municipality,
including, garbage classification, collection, and recycling;
c. add more waste recycling facilities; and
d. focus on the recycling methods of the recyclables.

We recommended that the LCE to: a) create a Disposal Committee to take charge on the proper mode of disposal of unserviceable assets b) require the Supply Officer to submit to the Disposal Committee the Inventory and Inspection of Unserviceable Property and Waste Material Report. It is further recommended that upon disposal of property, the pertinent portions of the reports shall be accomplished and that the supply Office provide the Accountant a copy thereof which shall be the basis for dropping the property from the books of accounts and for taking up the proceeds, if any from the sale of property.
observations.managementAction: Committee created.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

- Direct the Municipal Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical
count of PPE and come up with the RPCPPE.
- Direct the General Services Officer-designate and the Municipal
Accountant to reconcile their respective records and effect any
adjustments, if any.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant:

- To comply with the provisions of Sections 74 and 111 of PD No. 1445 in
the preparation of monthly bank reconciliation statements for all bank
accounts maintained by the Municipality and make written representations
with the depository banks to ensure prompt submission of the bank
statements.
- To draw the necessary journal entries to correct errors that affects the Cash
in Bank accounts.
- To return the outstanding checks for more than two (2) years back in the
books as debit to cash in bank and credit prior year’s adjustment duly
supported with necessary required documents.
- Coordinate with the concerned bank on the possibility of availing the
bank’s online facility to view and print the agency’s bank statements for a
given period.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to
expedite the processing of documents for the titling of lands owned by the
Municipality and come up with an appraised value of the land. And before any
construction is made, land ownership should be established by way of a
certificate of land title.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MPDO and the
Municipal Engineer to properly plan the implementation of the developmental
projects and ensure that all problems identified with the projects are resolved
before their inclusion to the priority development projects of the Municipality.
Also direct the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate enough fund for the
complete implementation of the identified projects and refrain from
appropriating funds on a staggered basis and ensures that only projects aligned
with the purpose/objectives of the 20% DF be funded from such
appropriation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local Risk Reduction
Management Council to prepare its risk reduction and management plan in
accordance with the provision of Section 5.0 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 dated March 25, 2013. And compel the
Municipal Budget Officer to charge disbursements out of the 70% of LDRRMF
only to those that are specifically used to support the risk reduction and
management activities of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC Chairman,
Municipal Engineer, MPDC, Municipal Agricultural Officer and Municipal
Health Officer to judiciously plan the implementation of projects funded by
the NGAs and monitor their completion to provide constituents of the benefits
due them.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant
and Municipal Treasurer to prepare a disbursement voucher in all loan
repayments. Likewise, the ADA and SOA should also be attached as
supporting documents to the disbursement voucher aside from the updated bank
statement.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to
formulate strategies to increase its income from the operation of its commercial
building.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the SWM Focal Person to
consider the appropriate means of communication depending on the information
that it wishes to pass on to the public and its stakeholders. For instance, there
should be public notices on television and on the radio about who can access the
Residual Containment Area (RCA), the working hours at the RCA to limit after-
hours access and the schedule for collection of wastes from households for the
different zones. Websites have become increasingly effective and economical in
disseminating information. Therefore it is important to have a specific link for
SWM within the Municipality's website so that it is easier for the public to have
access to information and policy on solid waste management as well as any
issue concerning solid waste management.

We recommended that a mechanism to measure the volume of waste and type
of waste should be placed at the site, which will provide baseline data to assist
in developing effective waste minimization strategies and future plans for
waste management.

We recommended that a proper gate at the entrance to the RCA must be built so
that it is easier for the personnel at the landfill to stop vehicles coming to the
RCA at the gate, inspect the waste to confirm the type of waste to be disposed
then direct the vehicle to the designated area depending on the type of waste
already inspected. This daily process will compensate for the non-segregation of
waste at source and will ensure that wastes being disposed at the RCA are
properly separated.

We further recommended that the SWM Focal Person should implement a color
coding or appropriate means of identification to ensure that wastes are properly
segregated at the RCA.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Solid Management
Program Focal Person to maintain disposal site records in the Residual
Containment Area.

We recommended that the RCA be fenced with a secured gate at the entrance
to minimize illegal visitors or scavengers. This will also help address the
occupational health and safety issues by limiting access.

We recommended that the Mayor direct the SWM Focal Person to ensure that
any person entering the RCA must wear the proper safety clothing and that the
operations at the RCA are effective and safe.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the HRMO to adhere to the
provisions of Section 77 of RA 7160 which provides that the Municipality may
hire Job-order for local projects only authorized by the Sangguniang Bayan and
to review the manning compliment of the Municipality to address the current
workload.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer
and Municipal Accountant to exert effort to enforce immediate settlement of the
audit disallowances and charges and to ensure that all employees who are
retiring or transferring to other agencies shall first settle the disallowances and
charges for which they are liable in accordance with Section 7 of COA Circular
No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor compel the Municipal Accountant
to review the details of the Due from LGUs and Other Receivable accounts and
exert all efforts to locate records to prove the validity of claims from the
identified recipients of these funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to see
to it that programs, projects and activities are aligned with GAD-related
undertakings so that the intended beneficiaries are assured of the services,
protection and other assistance that may be provided.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should appropriately disclose
in the Notes to Financial Statements the details of the GAD appropriation
during the year and the corresponding expended amount for the activities,
projects and programs in order to provide adequate information significant to
the financial statements and provide information useful in evaluating the
entity's performance in terms of service cost, efficiency and accomplishments.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to a) analyze the accounts with balances that remained
unreconciled and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the account
balance and b) coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to identify the
employees with unremitted contributions to enable the Municipal government
to settle its obligations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to
ensure that all taxes withheld from the General Fund and all those from other
funds be remitted to the BIR intact.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant as well as the General Services Officer to reconcile their respective PPE
records regularly to reflect an accurate and realistic amount in the financial statements.

We recommended that the LCE to: a) create a Disposal Committee to take
charge on the proper mode of disposal of unserviceable assets and b) require the Supply Officer
to submit to the Disposal Committee the Inventory and Inspection of Unserviceable
Property and Waste Material Report.
It is further recommended that upon disposal of property, the pertinent
portions of the reports shall be accomplished and that the supply office provide the
Accountant a copy thereof which shall be the basis for dropping the property from
the books of accounts and for taking up the proceeds, if any from the sale of
property.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive compel the Municipal
Accountant to maintain special account for all loan proceeds from which separate
breakdown/reports for loan transactions shall be prepared.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Agriculture
Officer and Municipal Health Officer to fast-track the implementation of priority
programs, projects and activities funded by the BUB Fund and LGSF so as to provide
the constituents of the benefits due them.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and other local officials
concerned implement the programmed projects under the 20% Development Fund
and to optimally utilize such fund to help achieve desirable socio-economic
development and environmental outcome.
Further, it is recommended that a reliable budget in the Annual Investment
Plan be prepared so to make a substantive evaluation of the programs/projects of the
agency and use the 20% DF amount within the range of the budgeted amount. Also,
the management should refrain from paying loan amortizations through IRA
deductions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: (a) direct the Municipal
Budget Officer to strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 15 of Republic Act
9344 known as Juvenile Justice and Welfare Act of 2006 and appropriate the
required allocation of 1% of its IRA for the LCPC activities and b) cause the
creation of the Local Council for the Protection of Children and compel the members
of the said council to prepare the Annual Work and Financial Plan for the projects
and activities for the welfare and protection of juvenile children.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive compel the BAC to submit the
Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) as required under Section 12.2 of the
Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that the LCE compel the GAD Focal Person to strictly
adhere to the guidelines in the preparation of Annual Gender and Development
(GAD) Plan and Budget and have it reviewed and approved by the DILG Regional
Office. Likewise, submit to the Audit Team GAD Accomplishment Report based on
the reviewed and duly approved by the DILG GAD Plan and Budget to ensure that
the programs/activities/objects reflected thereat are in accordance with the existing
regulations and guidelines.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: (a) provide the necessary
support to realize the sanitary landfill project (b) consider embarking on effective
projects and programs to support the LGUs ecological solid waste management
program and (c) strictly comply with all other prescribed procedures and guidelines
under RA No. 9003.

Engineer and MPDO to fast-track the implementation of programs and projects

the MOA and require them to submit the Liquidation Reports and other documents

be paid instead from the General Fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant as well as
the General Services Officer to reconcile their respective PPE records regularly to
reflect an accurate and realistic amount in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the BAC to submit the
aforementioned lacking supporting documents in order to be compliant with RA 9184 and
COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive directs the BAC to require the contractors
to issue another warranty that is compliant to the provisions of the IRR of RA 9184. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant must see to it that the correct amount of warranty
security which is 30% of the total contract price be imposed if surety bond is used for the
same future transactions.

We recommended that: a) the programs/projects included in the agency’s Annual
Investment Plan for the 20% Development Fund should be in consonance with the
development projects listed under the DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 s.
2011 dated April 13, 2011 and; b) the projects/programs of the barangay under the 20%
development fund should be indicated in the AIP of the LGU.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, being the chair of the Local School Board
strictly observe the provisions of Section 272 and Section 100 (c) of RA 7160 and that the
utilization of the proceeds of the Special Education Fund be limited to the prioritized
projects, programs and activities mandated therein.

We recommended for the strict adherence to the mandates on continuous disaster
monitoring as part of its management of LDRRMF and in the process, use available
resources towards the implementation of projects, programs and activities that are necessary
for the public to ensure readiness of the LGU during emergencies. Further, we recommended
for the utilization of the unexpended balance for disaster risk reduction and management
program and activities.

We recommended that the BAC prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR)
as required under Section 12.2 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 and the form prescribed by
the GPPB.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: (a) provide the necessary support to
establish MRF at the municipal level and implement waste segregation at source; (b) strictly
enforce and intensify the information campaign on the no-littering policy among the
residents; and (c) consider embarking on effective projects and programs to support the
LGUs ecological solid waste management program; (d) strictly comply with all other
prescribed procedures and guidelines under RA No. 9003.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant- Designate to maintain employee’s ledger for GSIS contributions/deductions to support the balances in the subsidiary and general ledgers and regularly reconcile the amounts withheld for remitted to GSIS in order to ascertain the accuracy and reliability of the balances of Due to GSIS in the financial statements pursuant to Sec. 111 (2) of PD 1445.
